title: melampyrum roseum subsp. ovalifolium
intro: Melampyrum roseum subsp. ovalifolium, also known as the oval-leaved monkey flower, is a plant species in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a hemiparasitic plant, meaning it obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of other plants, but also has the ability to photosynthesize like other plants. The oval-leaved monkey flower has been used in traditional medicine, with its roots and leaves said to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and fight infections. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, and is often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties.
